title: Dortmanna dregeana
intro: Dortmanna dregeana, also known as the Cape buttonbush, is a shrub-like plant belonging to the family Rutaceae, characterized by its small, white, star-shaped flowers and fragrant foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna dregeana include flavonoids, limonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Dortmanna dregeana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lythraceae. It belongs to the genus Dortmanna, which is a small group of plants.
